title: Datadog PHP Extension Installation

activity_flow.action: main() — guard: DD_TEST_EXECUTION env → return early if set

parse_validate_user_options() — calls parse_cli_arguments() to tokenise argv; normalises OPT_PHP_BIN, OPT_INSTALL_DIR (default /opt/datadog/dd-library), OPT_EXTENSION_DIR, OPT_ENABLE_APPSEC, OPT_ENABLE_PROFILING, OPT_FILE, OPT_PHP_INI, OPT_UNINSTALL
Dispatch on command: CMD_CONFIG_GET → cmd_config_get | CMD_CONFIG_SET → cmd_config_set | CMD_CONFIG_LIST → cmd_config_list | --uninstall → uninstall | default → install
install() — detect platform: get_architecture() + is_alpine() → build platform string (e.g. x86_64-linux-gnu)

install() — library prerequisite checks: check_library_prerequisite_or_exit('libcurl'), check_library_prerequisite_or_exit('libgcc_s'); if !alpine && enable-profiling: also check libdl.so, libpthread, librt

install() — require_binaries_or_exit($options): if OPT_PHP_BIN empty → pick_binaries_interactive() after search_php_binaries(); if 'all' → search_php_binaries() non-shebang; else resolve_command_full_path(command); exit if none found

install() — PRE-LOOP over selected binaries: check_php_ext_prerequisite_or_exit(fullPath, 'json'); ini_values(fullPath) to get PHP_API, IS_DEBUG, THREAD_SAFETY, INI_SCANDIR, INI_MAIN, EXTENSION_DIR; derive extensionSuffix ('-debug', '-zts'); collect downloadVersions map
install() — Prepare tmp dir /tmp/dd-install; register shutdown cleanup (rm -rf); clean existing tmp contents
install() — Archive acquisition: if OPT_FILE set → use local file (warning printed); else download() from RELEASE_URL_PREFIX, retry without version-specific suffix on 404
install() — Extract archive: tar -xf (Linux/Mac) or 7z x (Windows without tar)
install() — Copy tracer source files: mkdir installDir/dd-trace-sources; cp -r tmp/trace/src → installDir
install() — Copy appsec assets (if archive contains appsec dir): cp lib/ + etc/ to installDir
install() — PER-BINARY installation loop: for each selectedBinary:
→ check_php_ext_prerequisite_or_exit(fullPath, 'json') again
→ determine extDir (OPT_EXTENSION_DIR or phpProperties[EXTENSION_DIR])
→ safe_copy_extension(traceExtPath, extDir/ddtrace.so): copy to .tmp then rename to avoid segfault on live PHP processes
→ if profiling .so exists in archive: safe_copy_extension(profilingExtPath, extDir/datadog-profiling.so)
→ if appsec .so exists in archive: safe_copy_extension(appsecExtPath, extDir/ddappsec.so)
→ determine INI file path(s): OPT_PHP_INI if set; else find_main_ini_files() → returns 98-ddtrace.ini in scan dir (or existing ini with extension=ddtrace); on Debian also apache2/conf.d variant
→ for each INI file: if not exists → create file (mkdir -p if needed); if exists → set up replacements (upgrade old hook names, update sources_path)

→ build $replacements map: extension=ddtrace line, optionally profiling extension, optionally appsec extension + helper_path + rules path; if --enable-appsec → set datadog.appsec.enabled=On; if --enable-profiling but not supported → exit(1)
→ add_missing_ini_settings(iniFilePath, get_ini_settings(...), $replacements): apply regex replacements, append any missing settings
install() — Print SUCCESS; if interactive mode, print equivalent non-interactive command

key_functions.main: Entry point; parses options; dispatches to install/uninstall/config commands
key_functions.parse_validate_user_options: Calls parse_cli_arguments(); validates/normalises all CLI flags into structured $options array
key_functions.install: Core install orchestrator: platform detection → prerequisite checks → binary selection → archive download/extract → file copy → INI patching
key_functions.require_binaries_or_exit: Returns map of command→fullPath; interactive (pick_binaries_interactive) or explicit (--php-bin); exits if empty
key_functions.check_library_prerequisite_or_exit: On Alpine: find .so in /usr/local/lib,/usr/lib; on Linux: ldconfig -p | grep; exits if not found
key_functions.check_php_ext_prerequisite_or_exit: Runs php -m; warns if output unreliable; exits if named extension absent
key_functions.safe_copy_extension: copy(src, dst.tmp) then rename(dst.tmp, dst) — avoids segfault on live-loaded .so; on Windows moves old file to TEMP first
key_functions.find_main_ini_files: Resolves 98-ddtrace.ini path in scan dir; detects Debian SAPI split (cli/apache2/fpm); falls back to INI_MAIN
key_functions.add_missing_ini_settings: Reads INI file, applies regex replacements, appends any settings not yet present
key_functions.download: Tries ext-curl → CLI curl → file_get_contents; 404 retry logic for version-specific archives

key_conditions: IS_WINDOWS: different paths, commands, and archive extraction (7z vs tar)
is_alpine(): use find /usr/local/lib instead of ldconfig for library checks
OPT_PHP_BIN=all → search all PHP binaries on PATH
OPT_FILE set → skip download, use local .tar.gz
INI_SCANDIR missing → warn about fragile install; fall back to INI_MAIN
profiling .so exists in archive → copy; else if --enable-profiling → exit(1)
appsec .so exists in archive → copy; else if --enable-appsec → exit(1)
Single downloadVersion → version-specific archive download suffix; multiple → full archive

data_objects.options: Normalised CLI options: OPT_PHP_BIN[], OPT_INSTALL_DIR, OPT_EXTENSION_DIR, OPT_PHP_INI[], OPT_FILE, OPT_UNINSTALL, OPT_ENABLE_APPSEC, OPT_ENABLE_PROFILING
data_objects.phpProperties: Output of ini_values(binary): PHP_API, PHP_VER, IS_DEBUG, THREAD_SAFETY, INI_SCANDIR, INI_MAIN, EXTENSION_DIR
data_objects.selectedBinaries: Map command → fullPath for PHP binaries to target
data_objects.replacements: Regex → replacement map applied to INI file during patching

exit_paths: print_error_and_exit + exit(1): library missing, PHP ext missing, no binaries, no scan dir+ini, unsupported profiling/appsec build, archive extract fail, INI write fail
important_notes: The installer is a single self-contained PHP script. Default install dir is /opt/datadog (Windows: %ProgramW6432%\Datadog\PHP Tracer). safe_copy_extension uses copy+rename (not direct copy) to prevent segfault in running PHP processes. Three extension modules: ddtrace (tracer), datadog-profiling, ddappsec. INI file default name is 98-ddtrace.ini. Profiling prerequisites (libdl, libpthread, librt) only checked on non-Alpine Linux. DD_TEST_EXECUTION env bypasses all execution.
